events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: concrete placement in progress, observed segregation from excessive drop height. This is a sequence/procedure violation. ACI 304 limits drop height to avoid segregation (typically max 3-4 feet for unconfined). Excessive drop causes segregation, honeycombing, defective concrete. Work must stop, drop height corrected, and affected concrete may need removal/rework.

Decision: fail (non-compliant, defective placement).
Risk: high.
Work stage: in-progress.
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.
Percent complete: maybe 40.
Value: 0 (no numeric reading asked).
Confidence: 0.9.
Actions: stop placement, correct drop height (use chutes, pumps, tremie), evaluate/remove segregated concrete.
References: ACI 301, ACI 304.

"actions":["Stop concrete placement immediately","Correct delivery method to limit free-fall drop height using chutes, buggies, pump, or tremie","Inspect and probe already-placed segregated concrete for honeycombing and paste/aggregate separation","Remove and replace defective segregated concrete before proceeding","Resume placement only after drop-height control is verified and accepted"],
"rationale":"ACI 304 requires concrete to be placed so segregation is prevented, limiting free-fall drop height for unconfined placement.
